title: SDN Controller High Availability
spl:
index=sdn sourcetype="sdn:controller_ha" earliest=-24h
| where quorum_ok=0 OR leader_id!=expected_leader
| stats latest(quorum_ok) as q, latest(leader_id) as leader by cluster_name
| table cluster_name, q, leader
description: Quorum, leader election, and data-store sync for controller clusters — extends generic health (UC-18.3.3) with **HA-specific** failover signals.
value: Quorum, leader election, and data-store sync for controller clusters — extends generic health (UC-18.3.3) with **HA-specific** failover signals.
grandmaExplanation: Quorum, leader election, and data-store sync for controller clusters — extends generic health (UC-18.3.3) with **HA-specific** failover signals — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
implementation: Map `expected_leader` from static config lookup. Alert on quorum loss or rogue leader.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `sdn:controller`, OpenDaylight, ONOS (custom).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sdn:controller_ha`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map `expected_leader` from static config lookup. Alert on quorum loss or rogue leader.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=sdn sourcetype="sdn:controller_ha" earliest=-24h
| where quorum_ok=0 OR leader_id!=expected_leader
| stats latest(quorum_ok) as q, latest(leader_id) as leader by cluster_name
| table cluster_name, q, leader
```

Understanding this SPL

**SDN Controller High Availability** — Quorum, leader election, and data-store sync for controller clusters — extends generic health (UC-18.3.3) with **HA-specific** failover signals.

Documented **Data sources**: `sdn:controller_ha`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `sdn:controller`, OpenDaylight, ONOS (custom). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: sdn; **sourcetype**: sdn:controller_ha. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=sdn, sourcetype="sdn:controller_ha", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where quorum_ok=0 OR leader_id!=expected_leader` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Pipeline stage (see **SDN Controller High Availability**): table cluster_name, q, leader


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (cluster), Timeline (failover events), Single value (cluster up).
visualization: Status grid (cluster), Timeline (failover events), Single value (cluster up).
